mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing within one month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear outcomes with an accuracy above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates rising capacity utilization and margin expansion, prompting short-term equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during recovery phases when industrial production accelerates, or the signal shows zero predictive power outside recession troughs.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate erodes real yields and signals impending monetary tightening, causing investors to discount future earnings and drive negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3% while FEDFUNDS change = 0
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite inflation surprises when rates are held steady, or the interaction effect reverses direction in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 900,000 units annually indicates a collapse in construction demand and related supply chains, dragging down corporate revenues and producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances when housing starts breach the 900k threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports near-term consumer confidence and generates positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth, or the signal produces a negative mean return across rolling 1-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing industrial production and rising GDP growth signals a shift toward services-led expansion, reducing cyclical equity exposure and triggering negative returns for broad market indices over one month.
failure_condition: Broad market indices continue to rally during services-led growth phases, or the divergence signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).
